item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i deadass love this idea but teleporting/reality bending makes it feel cheap… if it commits to a 'harder' physics approach with goofy fluid behaviour then it can stand to be a lot more cohesive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Throughout the whole article, I was thinking about ways for it to be contained effectively.

Basically, have an intricate array of pipes that shifts at regular intervals, or if sensors detect the anomaly. This is to be programmed to create a sequence of pipe with one exit, that requires the anomaly to travel a distance as large as possible to get to the end. Once it gets to a certain proximity to the exit, the maze changes to make the length considerably longer. At no time is the loop to be closed.

I thought of this after determining that it is like any liquid, in that it takes the path of least resistance. Teleportation is only noted after containment procedures remove all possibility of escape by other means. If escape is technically possible by non-teleporting, then it seems likely that it would try to go for that. Depending on the intelligence of the juice, it could take a long time for it to figure it out and teleport.

Another possibility is to define its containment as all of space and time, with the exception of a certain holding cell.

Also, I seem to recall an SCP object that dispenses any liquid entered into the keypad. Perhaps entering in SCP-3453, or Contained 3453, could be part of the procedures.

I realize this is a joke, but I couldn’t stop thinking about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the concept of SCPs that exist solely to aggravate the foundation and this was nothing short of fun to read

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh shoot, I'm actually quite disappointed in myself for not realizing this. Thanks for catching this Gibbon Man! I believe I have fixed the issue by dividing the average containment time by 3 and the number of containment attempts by 3 as well. So it should be a ~10 year timeline, beginning at 2008. According to a webpage I found, I believe the Elementary school to be founded in 2006. So it should work out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: did no one else do the math on this thing? it would have to have been in containment for around 90 years for these numbers to be correct, yet the uni it was found originally at is not that old. a strong -1 for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I believe they're referring to SCP-3284, which, I agree does the "uncontainable silly thing" better.

I was initially interested in the constantly-in-motion-juice and why it's so hard to contain, but giving it the ability to teleport when it can't actually escape feels cheap and boring.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was actually unaware that this concept has been done before. I tried searching for "ball bearing SCP" but the only thing of note that I could find was SCP-669, which presumably isn't what you're referring to…

Could you provide me with the SCP number of this one?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've giving it a -1. The only part I liked was trying to get it to destroy itself by claiming its containment was "reality".

It's an interesting idea; but the ball bearing Skip does it better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This page has been rewritten by yours truly.
I'd like to thank TL333s and PeppersGhost for their help.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're right, sorry, I was being a drama queen and over reacted. I shouldn't have gotten worked up over it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is glorifying either an unconvicted murderer or racism, depending on your opinion. -1

/s

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nah, fam. SCP ded.

Edit: autocorrect doesn't know fam is a word.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dude, we've had mediocre/deeply flawed articles around here since the dawn of time. It's okay to dislike the article, but let's not act like this is some harbinger of the end-times. The sky isn't falling just because another off-tone SCP managed to get to the low positives.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I probably should be less inflammatory, but the wiki can go fuck itself if the future of articles is like this, they probably need to raise the age limit again if this is really what people consider a good article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would definitely use less inflammatory language, but this thread covers my reaction to this article quite thoroughly.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: ah okay. I was unaware.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
good joke

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If it's a direct quote from a kid they interviewed on site, then it should be in the article as a direct quote from a kid they interviewed on site, not just jammed in without context as a first person recount.

The joke tries too hard, I find the reality containment thing stupid, it's not consistent in any way, it even ends with the Hubble seeing the juice in space ignoring how the Foundation has any indication how to know where to point a telescope to see this thing as it pops back into reality, the article just drops that like it's not weird outside of the fact that the Juice popped back into the universe, and that the fact that the Hubble was pointed at just the right area of space to see this thing and with resolution is normal.

Everything in this article is a lazily jammed in for the sake of the joke, I don't know how anyone could think this is written well in any way, it is barely better formatted than my comments.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Author, I've cleared the tags. If you're not going to add all the tags for your article, it's best to just leave the page untagged and wait for an experienced tagger to take care of it. We have a team of people on the site who tag articles, and they search for pages that have no tags. Adding only a few tags prevents the team from finding the article, so if you're unfamiliar with the tags, it's totally fine to wait for them to take care of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I disagree. Like, here's your first example: SCP-3453 then jumped up in the air, performed 3 loops, and moved erratically for 2 hours before going down a sewer drain

That reads to me like a direct quote from some excited kid they interviewed on site. Since the foundation didn't personally see it, they have only that available to them for documentation of the incident. It feels fine to me.

I really enjoy the twist (get it? get it?) of the OJ
+ show block
– hide block

noping out of reality when told reality was containing it.

That really sold its sentience (and intransigence) to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is cool and all but it kinda begs the question why it didn't just go back to what it was doing pre-breach. And in really short things unclear parts of the plot shine brighter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article needs rewriting for clinical tone and language, for example:

SCP-3453 then jumped up in the air, performed 3 loops, and moved erratically for 2 hours before going down a sewer drain.

This shouldn't be written as a first person perspective if no one from the Foundation observed it, it should take into account that this data was gathered from interviews with students, so even just adding 'Students observed' and changing the tense to reflect the change in view would be a big improvement.

Later on in the same paragraph you refer to the student who had the original orange juice as the 'wielder'. No one says that someone wields a carton or bottle or OJ they are intending to drink, maybe if they were going to beat someone to death or something, and that's still stretching it.

The article reads like one big run-on sentence, it's just basically SCP -3453 does X, SCP -3453 Y, Z and seems to exist only for the sake of the joke implied in the article title, which isn't enough to justify the existence of a skip without some meat behind it. The 'Safety Dance' is an example of an article that did it well.

-1 pending a rewrite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy shit, I remember the original, original post of this from way back. Didn't think i'd see it again. The Juice truly is loose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The juice runs rampant, and cannot be stopped. It once tried non-existence, but found it too boring.
